title: v0.1 Alpha Release
description: Shipped across the v0.1.x releases. Gate for making the repo public and sharing with coworkers. Language surface is honest — named arguments (#56), agents nest in modules (#63), types usable from agents (#70), enum variant construction completeness (#96), capability checks cover test blocks (#104) — schedule handlers fire on their own (#71), agent events survive crashes (#72), every spec example compiles or is marked Planned (#77), the first-five-minutes DX holds up (assertion failures say why #105, skein new git-inits #106, zsh completion #101, MCP compile_check tells the truth #109), and releases/badges/versioned docs are automated for public consumption (#100).

title: v0.2 Beta Release
description: Shipped as v0.2.0. Post-alpha hardening, informed by alpha feedback: replay backend injection (#73), the capability-parameter surface decision (#69) and parameter-scoped enforcement for process/timer/event.log (#57), process.spawn task bodies (#74), enum value-level exhaustiveness warnings (#76), local LLM backends for dev via OpenAI-compatible servers + skein.toml profiles (#107), LSP code actions from fix_hint/fix_code (#108), and the Int string-interpolation fix (#114).

title: v1.0.0-rc Release
description: The gate for tagging v1.0.0-rc.1 — output of the 2026-06-11 release-readiness pass. Shipped: every item fixed and v1.0.0-rc.1 tagged 2026-06-12. NOTE (2026-06-15 roadmap reset): rc.1 was tagged prematurely. The 2026-06-14/15 dogfooding audit (skein-testing + FablePool ports) reset the GA picture — v1.0.0 is no longer an imminent RC promotion. The next release is the development milestone v0.4.0, not another RC. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: v0.4.0 — Soundness & scenario capability environments
description: First reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave 0: stop RC-promotion framing; make docs/spec truthful (no imminent-GA claims, no `via` as accepted design). Wave 1: type/runtime soundness (no `:unknown` at public boundaries; record/map structural checking; one Result representation across every effect boundary; golden replay actually activates) — mostly landed, finished and pinned by a negative-fixture corpus. Wave 2: scenario-scoped capability environments — nested `capability tool.use(T) { capability http.out(...) { implement(...) } }` envelopes (replacing the `via` design), tool effect-summary analysis, provider purity, a runtime dynamic capability stack, and analyzer-enforced test purity (effects only in `scenario`, never `test`). Design: docs/design/scenario-capability-environments.md.

title: v0.5.0 — Observability, canonical substrate & conformance
description: Second reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave 3: deterministic scenario/golden/replay gate — scenario-local store/memory/event isolation, controlled uuid/instant/llm providers, http blocked unless implemented/replayed/explicitly allowed, structured mismatch diagnostics. Wave 4: CLI human/agent observability — pure framework-neutral trace renderer (fix the `skein trace` crash), byte-stable plain output, JSON output schemas for check/test/trace, a TTY/TUI gate that never breaks MCP/LSP/non-TTY protocols. Wave 5: minimal canonical substrate (spec the canonical bytes first + cross-impl vectors, then Bytes/hash/hex/base64/verify-only crypto, String.join/codepoints, Int modulo/bitwise) — included iff `FablePool-capable` stays in the 1.0 pitch (open decision). Wave 6: dogfood conformance gate — CI compiles, loads, and RUNS reduced programs from Skein/skein-testing/FablePool-skein plus the negative-fixture corpus.

title: v1.0.0-rc.2 — True release candidate
description: Cut a real RC only once every 1.0 blocker in v0.4.0 + v0.5.0 is green and the conformance gate executes the reduced testbeds end-to-end. This replaces the premature rc.1 framing: no new RC until the new 1.0 gate is close to green. Soak before promoting to v1.0.0.

title: v1.0.0 Release
description: The GA tag — NOT imminent (reset 2026-06-15). GA is a sound, honest, testable, observable core: (1) sound type/runtime core; (2) typed/honest capabilities & effects; (3) scenario-scoped capability environments; (4) deterministic/replayable scenario/golden testing; (5) CLI human/agent observability; (6) minimal canonical bytes/hash substrate (if FablePool-capable is claimed); (7) dogfood conformance across Skein, skein-testing, and FablePool-skein. Tags once v1.0.0-rc.2 has soaked and every blocker is closed. The earlier 'rc soaks while docs findings land, then promote' plan is superseded.

title: v1.1: Hardening & Language
description: Post-1.0 language + effect work intentionally cut from the minimal 1.0 surface: closures/anonymous functions (#248), `via`/`via Module` if still useful after scenario environments, behavioural stateful stubs (store/memory/event fault injection), general effect-provider interfaces, content-addressed store (#255), effectful crypto — secure RNG/keygen/signing (#257), `llm.rerank` (#145), docs/spec drift guards (#202), and the LSP structural-navigation work (#240). Language ergonomics surfaced by dogfooding (record update #251, module constants #249, enum variant payloads #247, Int/String stdlib gaps #246/#250) land here unless the conformance suite proves a specific one a hard 1.0 blocker.

title: v1.2: Interop & Agent Workflows
description: Bigger design efforts after the hardening wave: Erlang/Elixir FFI via extern (#141), human-in-the-loop approval workflows with suspend before sensitive tool calls (#144), the browser-based trace viewer (#143), the Raxol TUI for an interactive CLI (#171, separately gated from the framework-neutral CLI work), and native structural search/codemod (#241).

title: Future: Platform
description: Deliberately deferred platform bets, parked until the core language and runtime stabilize: hot code upgrades (#142), the managed deployment platform (#148), and the tools/connectors marketplace (#149). Re-scope each from scratch when pulled into active work.
